name: Camp Kennebec
address: 1422 Cox Rd Central Frontenac, ON, K0H 1B0 (178km) Clipboard
description: Residential camp for children and adults with special needs group rentals during off season camp runs one to eight week programs programs are adaptable to campers if and when they need it wide range of activities available leadership training Waterfront games, sports and skills: swimming waterskiing water trampoline wakeboarding tubing canoeing kayaking paddle boards Expert instruction and skills building in land activities: horseback riding high ropes zip lining archery land sports trips and camp-outs Creative and artistic options: music wood shop arts and crafts dance drama NOTE: Open to campers from all locations.

address: 365 Evans Ave, Suite 202 Toronto, ON, M8Z 1K2 (411km) Clipboard
description: Provides resources, services, information and advocacy for children, youth and adults with learning disabilities and/or ADHD * supporting parents, teachers and other professionals * encourages research

name: Ontario. Ministry of Education
address: Trillium Demonstration School, Building H; 347 Ontario St S Milton, ON, L9T 3X9 (441km) Clipboard
description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

address: Clipboard
description: Residential treatment program: short- and long-term residential treatment for young men in the Ottawa area using a behaviourally-based treatment of those with emotional, social or behavioural problems without limitation to specific profiles or exclusion specialized treatment plans for each resident to ensure comfort, security, personal growth and optimal treatment gains Adaptive Daily Living: offers individuals 18 years and over the opportunity to acquire life, social, vocational, leisure and community integration skills serves a fixed number of individuals who benefit from intensive training in all of the above areas, along with direct job placement and coaching places of future employment Host Family Programs for adults with behavioral challenges, serves adults with a wide variety of needs, in a consistent structured manner, individualized day program with full supervision in various community-based programs, including exercise program, volunteering, job coaching, life skills, cooking, etc. Achievement Program: semi-independent living program for young men with a developmental delay incorporates one staff to three young men direct support for the facilitation and acquisition of life skills that promote healthy semi-independent living with greater independence

address: Trillium Demonstration School, Building H; 347 Ontario St S Milton, ON, L9T 3X9 (53km) Clipboard
description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

name: Learning Disabilities Association of Chatham-Kent
address: Chatham-Kent Secondary School, 285 McNaughton Ave E Chatham-Kent, ON, N7L 2G7 (2km) Clipboard
description: One-to-one tutoring for elementary aged children using individualized programs. SOAR - a secondary school transition program in a small group focusing on learning strategies, compensatory strategies, and self-advocacy skills. Social Skills program for elementary aged children. Group sessions assist children in the development of social skills and social problem-solving strategies so that they can interact successfully with others. Advocacy, information and referral are available with availability of a resource library of books, videos, children's books, information meetings, and newsletter.

address: Trillium Demonstration School, Building H; 347 Ontario St S Milton, ON, L9T 3X9 (225km) Clipboard
description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

name: Northumberland Child Development Centre
address: 205 Peter St Port Hope, ON, L1A 3V6 (8km) Clipboard
description: Mosaic Minds provides integrative, therapeutic services for neurodiverse children and youth with a focus on supporting sensory, emotional, social and developmental growth. Therapy offers a supportive environment where a child experiencing challenges with sensory processing, social communication, emotional regulation or developmental transitions can grow and thrive. Program staff are certified AutPlay Practitioners and certified Play Therapists. Each child's path begins with a Sensory Processing Assessment to identify individual sensory needs and areas of strength. Additional assessment builds a tailored therapy plan incorporating sensory integration techniques, emotional regulation strategies, AutPlay therapy and other developmentally appropriate modalities. The approach, grounded in current neurodevelopmental research, blends clinical expertise with child-centered care, creating a supportive and engaging therapeutic environment. The aim is to improve functional skills, increase self regulation, foster connection and help every child succeed.

description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

address: 9030 Leslie St, Ste 216 Richmond Hill, ON, L4B 1G2 (9km) Clipboard
description: Programs for children, youth, and adults with learning disabilities (LDs) and/or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) including parent and adult support groups, tutoring services, advocacy and information and referral Tutoring Volunteer tutors from different diverse and educational backgrounds work one-on-one with children in grades 4-8 on: supporting students with homework help recognizing comfortable learning styles, learning strengths, and study habits which assist children in better learning assisting children with numeracy and literacy skills which need further development Homework & Beyond ​ For students who need extra support completing homework and developing social skills during the school year; f acilitators meet with students once a week in a supportive group to: assist with homework completion and understanding. build social skills, such as making friends, self-advocacy, and addressing bullying provide a positive space for learning and personal growth Social Skills 6 week program focuses on social development skills; social coaches, who are registered social service workers, engage with students on a variety of social skills; program content is based on the PEERS® curriculum for social skills training Group meets once a week for one hour and discusses topics such as: interpersonal skills. i.e. communication, listening, friendship skills coping with emotions and stress conflict resolution and problem solving self advocacy and self esteem skills SOAR Transitions Elementary for students in grades 7-8 diagnosed with LD and/or ADHD assists in developing life skills to succeed academically and vocationally SOAR Transitions Secondary -- supports students in secondary schools diagnosed with learning disability or ADHD gain practical coping and advocacy skills in one to one and group sessions Strategies for Life one-on one program that focuses on the socio-emotional effects of LDs and Attention Deficit Disorder identifies unique challenges the participant may face and assists youth in developing resiliency. Summer Camp day camp for children with a primary diagnosis of Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD safe, encouraging and positive environment for learning social skills activities include outdoor swimming, nature walks, and arts and crafts Job Fit Program helps adults and/or youth with LD's and/or ADHD explore interests, strengths, and areas of need in order to pursue employment covers how to ask for accommodations in the workplace ensures clients have a full understanding of their LD's and develops client's confidence to join the workforce Parent Support Group virtual support group to help guide caregivers through different stages of accepting and managing their child's Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD monthly opportunity to meet and share common experiences, handle emotions, strategize for strengths and challenges in a positive way Adult Support Group opportunity to network with individuals with LD's and/or ADHD, gain social support, problem solve and share successes. learn different strategies to deal with and manage LDs and/or ADHD.

description: Provides resources, services, information and advocacy for children, youth and adults with learning disabilities and/or ADHD * supporting parents, teachers and other professionals * encourages research

name: Ontario. Ministry of Education
address: Trillium Demonstration School, Building H; 347 Ontario St S Milton, ON, L9T 3X9 (48km) Clipboard
description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

name: International Dyslexia Association - Ontario Branch
address: Clipboard
description: IDA Ontario is primarily volunteer-operated and provides a wide range of programs including: The Literacy and Learning Conference Workshops, webinars and teacher training courses Information & support for parents navigating the school system Website with bilingual resources for libraries, teachers, parents & students Resources and support for public libraries Reading Road Trip Podcast News & updates on literacy in Ontario

name: Camp Kennebec
address: 1422 Cox Rd Central Frontenac, ON, K0H 1B0 (220km) Clipboard

name: Genesis Residential Treatment Program
address: Clipboard
description: Residential treatment program: short- and long-term residential treatment for young men in the Ottawa area using a behaviourally-based treatment of those with emotional, social or behavioural problems without limitation to specific profiles or exclusion specialized treatment plans for each resident to ensure comfort, security, personal growth and optimal treatment gains Adaptive Daily Living: offers individuals 18 years and over the opportunity to acquire life, social, vocational, leisure and community integration skills serves a fixed number of individuals who benefit from intensive training in all of the above areas, along with direct job placement and coaching places of future employment Host Family Programs for adults with behavioral challenges, serves adults with a wide variety of needs, in a consistent structured manner, individualized day program with full supervision in various community-based programs, including exercise program, volunteering, job coaching, life skills, cooking, etc. Achievement Program: semi-independent living program for young men with a developmental delay incorporates one staff to three young men direct support for the facilitation and acquisition of life skills that promote healthy semi-independent living with greater independence

name: Chisholm Academy
address: 1484 Cornwall Rd Oakville, ON, L6J 7W5 (20km) Clipboard
description: A special education school that caters to students with learning disabilities such as dyslexia, ADHD and executive functioning challenges Academic programs and services include: private senior elementary school private high school individualized educational programs (IEP) academic upgrading for students and adults with learning disabilities Affiliated with the Chisholm Assessment Centre providing educational assessments and counselling services for elementary, secondary and post-secondary students

name: Oakville Public Library
address:
Oakville Public Library, Central Branch
120 Navy St Oakville, ON, L6J 2Z4 (24km) Clipboard
description: Centre for Equitable Library Access (CELA) Resources through a partnership with the CNIB/CELA library, access the collections and services of the CNIB/CELA library collections include Braille, Talking Books and magazines, electronic text and descriptive videos available at Woodside Branch Connection Kits offering two types of connection kits, a Tablet + Wi-Fi Kit or an Accessible Reading Tablet these kits are to help Oakville residents who are 55+ and do not have access to the internet or technology, stay connected with friends and family and to access alternate formats of reading material for those with print disabilities to learn more, please call your local branch for more information DAISY Disc an audio book that uses the international DAISY (Digital Accessible Information System) Standard available at Woodside Branch Delivery by Mail if you are unable to come into the library in person, you may be eligible for OPL's Delivery Service option please email oplbymail@oakville.ca or contact your local branch to find out if you qualify restricted to a maximum of two (2) items per package, 2 packages per month per customer Large Print Books popular fiction and non-fiction books in large print format search catalogue for current large print items available at all branches MagnusCards the MagnusCards app is designed to help individuals with autism or other special needs interact independently and with confidence in the Oakville community users can follow guides to library services by downloading the app for free through the App Store or on Google Play ReachDeck Toolbar makes the OPL website more accessible with powerful features READING AIDS Kurzweil Reading Machines computers equipped with scanners and specialized software. They allow you to scan printed material, magnify it on the screen, and then have the text read aloud to you, available at Woodside and Central Branch ZoomText a screen magnification software that allows customers to view the library catalogue and the internet in large print, available at Woodside and Central Branch SmartView a magnifier that enhances all reading materials (magazines, newspapers, photographs, bills), available at Woodside Branch Victor Readers / DAISY Player also known as DAISY players, are used for listening to digital talking books, available at Woodside Branch

description: Support organization for parents and caregivers of children with learning disabilities represents parents and their children in various fields in education, especially early childhood education raises awareness among parents and educators about learning disorders (dyslexia, dysorthography, dyscalculia, dyspraxia, dysphasia, attention deficit disorder with or without hyperactivity (ADD/ADHD) by providing information about: the symptoms linked to the different forms and levels of dyslexia and attention deficit with or without hyperactivity different teaching strategies and adaptive methods community resources encourages early detection of learning disabilities support to parents by phone, by email, and through virtual meetings, such as conferences (virtually by ZOOM) information and sharing sessions (virtually by ZOOM) training sessions for parents

name: Camp Kennebec
address: 1422 Cox Rd Central Frontenac, ON, K0H 1B0 (192km) Clipboard
description: Residential camp for children and adults with special needs group rentals during off season camp runs one to eight week programs programs are adaptable to campers if and when they need it wide range of activities available leadership training Waterfront games, sports and skills: swimming waterskiing water trampoline wakeboarding tubing canoeing kayaking paddle boards Expert instruction and skills building in land activities: horseback riding high ropes zip lining archery land sports trips and camp-outs Creative and artistic options: music wood shop arts and crafts dance drama NOTE: Open to campers from all locations.

address: 365 Evans Ave, Suite 202 Toronto, ON, M8Z 1K2 (192km) Clipboard

name: Ontario. Ministry of Education
address: Trillium Demonstration School, Building H; 347 Ontario St S Milton, ON, L9T 3X9 (208km) Clipboard
description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

description: Support organization for parents and caregivers of children with learning disabilities represents parents and their children in various fields in education, especially early childhood education raises awareness among parents and educators about learning disorders (dyslexia, dysorthography, dyscalculia, dyspraxia, dysphasia, attention deficit disorder with or without hyperactivity (ADD/ADHD) by providing information about: the symptoms linked to the different forms and levels of dyslexia and attention deficit with or without hyperactivity different teaching strategies and adaptive methods community resources encourages early detection of learning disabilities support to parents by phone, by email, and through virtual meetings, such as conferences (virtually by ZOOM) information and sharing sessions (virtually by ZOOM) training sessions for parents

name: Ontario. Ministry of Education
address: Trillium Demonstration School, Building H; 347 Ontario St S Milton, ON, L9T 3X9 (315km) Clipboard
description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

name: Learning Disabilities Association of York-Simcoe
address: 9030 Leslie St, Ste 216 Richmond Hill, ON, L4B 1G2 (4km) Clipboard
description: Programs for children, youth, and adults with learning disabilities (LDs) and/or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) including parent and adult support groups, tutoring services, advocacy and information and referral Tutoring Volunteer tutors from different diverse and educational backgrounds work one-on-one with children in grades 4-8 on: supporting students with homework help recognizing comfortable learning styles, learning strengths, and study habits which assist children in better learning assisting children with numeracy and literacy skills which need further development Homework & Beyond ​ For students who need extra support completing homework and developing social skills during the school year; f acilitators meet with students once a week in a supportive group to: assist with homework completion and understanding. build social skills, such as making friends, self-advocacy, and addressing bullying provide a positive space for learning and personal growth Social Skills 6 week program focuses on social development skills; social coaches, who are registered social service workers, engage with students on a variety of social skills; program content is based on the PEERS® curriculum for social skills training Group meets once a week for one hour and discusses topics such as: interpersonal skills. i.e. communication, listening, friendship skills coping with emotions and stress conflict resolution and problem solving self advocacy and self esteem skills SOAR Transitions Elementary for students in grades 7-8 diagnosed with LD and/or ADHD assists in developing life skills to succeed academically and vocationally SOAR Transitions Secondary -- supports students in secondary schools diagnosed with learning disability or ADHD gain practical coping and advocacy skills in one to one and group sessions Strategies for Life one-on one program that focuses on the socio-emotional effects of LDs and Attention Deficit Disorder identifies unique challenges the participant may face and assists youth in developing resiliency. Summer Camp day camp for children with a primary diagnosis of Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD safe, encouraging and positive environment for learning social skills activities include outdoor swimming, nature walks, and arts and crafts Job Fit Program helps adults and/or youth with LD's and/or ADHD explore interests, strengths, and areas of need in order to pursue employment covers how to ask for accommodations in the workplace ensures clients have a full understanding of their LD's and develops client's confidence to join the workforce Parent Support Group virtual support group to help guide caregivers through different stages of accepting and managing their child's Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD monthly opportunity to meet and share common experiences, handle emotions, strategize for strengths and challenges in a positive way Adult Support Group opportunity to network with individuals with LD's and/or ADHD, gain social support, problem solve and share successes. learn different strategies to deal with and manage LDs and/or ADHD.

description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

name: Oakville Public Library
address:
Oakville Public Library, Central Branch
120 Navy St Oakville, ON, L6J 2Z4 (16km) Clipboard
description: Centre for Equitable Library Access (CELA) Resources through a partnership with the CNIB/CELA library, access the collections and services of the CNIB/CELA library collections include Braille, Talking Books and magazines, electronic text and descriptive videos available at Woodside Branch Connection Kits offering two types of connection kits, a Tablet + Wi-Fi Kit or an Accessible Reading Tablet these kits are to help Oakville residents who are 55+ and do not have access to the internet or technology, stay connected with friends and family and to access alternate formats of reading material for those with print disabilities to learn more, please call your local branch for more information DAISY Disc an audio book that uses the international DAISY (Digital Accessible Information System) Standard available at Woodside Branch Delivery by Mail if you are unable to come into the library in person, you may be eligible for OPL's Delivery Service option please email oplbymail@oakville.ca or contact your local branch to find out if you qualify restricted to a maximum of two (2) items per package, 2 packages per month per customer Large Print Books popular fiction and non-fiction books in large print format search catalogue for current large print items available at all branches MagnusCards the MagnusCards app is designed to help individuals with autism or other special needs interact independently and with confidence in the Oakville community users can follow guides to library services by downloading the app for free through the App Store or on Google Play ReachDeck Toolbar makes the OPL website more accessible with powerful features READING AIDS Kurzweil Reading Machines computers equipped with scanners and specialized software. They allow you to scan printed material, magnify it on the screen, and then have the text read aloud to you, available at Woodside and Central Branch ZoomText a screen magnification software that allows customers to view the library catalogue and the internet in large print, available at Woodside and Central Branch SmartView a magnifier that enhances all reading materials (magazines, newspapers, photographs, bills), available at Woodside Branch Victor Readers / DAISY Player also known as DAISY players, are used for listening to digital talking books, available at Woodside Branch

description: Provides resources, services, information and advocacy for children, youth and adults with learning disabilities and/or ADHD * supporting parents, teachers and other professionals * encourages research

name: Ontario. Ministry of Education
address: Trillium Demonstration School, Building H; 347 Ontario St S Milton, ON, L9T 3X9 (864km) Clipboard
description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

name: Learning Disabilities Association of York-Simcoe
address: 9030 Leslie St, Ste 216 Richmond Hill, ON, L4B 1G2 (24km) Clipboard
description: Programs for children, youth, and adults with learning disabilities (LDs) and/or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) including parent and adult support groups, tutoring services, advocacy and information and referral Tutoring Volunteer tutors from different diverse and educational backgrounds work one-on-one with children in grades 4-8 on: supporting students with homework help recognizing comfortable learning styles, learning strengths, and study habits which assist children in better learning assisting children with numeracy and literacy skills which need further development Homework & Beyond ​ For students who need extra support completing homework and developing social skills during the school year; f acilitators meet with students once a week in a supportive group to: assist with homework completion and understanding. build social skills, such as making friends, self-advocacy, and addressing bullying provide a positive space for learning and personal growth Social Skills 6 week program focuses on social development skills; social coaches, who are registered social service workers, engage with students on a variety of social skills; program content is based on the PEERS® curriculum for social skills training Group meets once a week for one hour and discusses topics such as: interpersonal skills. i.e. communication, listening, friendship skills coping with emotions and stress conflict resolution and problem solving self advocacy and self esteem skills SOAR Transitions Elementary for students in grades 7-8 diagnosed with LD and/or ADHD assists in developing life skills to succeed academically and vocationally SOAR Transitions Secondary -- supports students in secondary schools diagnosed with learning disability or ADHD gain practical coping and advocacy skills in one to one and group sessions Strategies for Life one-on one program that focuses on the socio-emotional effects of LDs and Attention Deficit Disorder identifies unique challenges the participant may face and assists youth in developing resiliency. Summer Camp day camp for children with a primary diagnosis of Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD safe, encouraging and positive environment for learning social skills activities include outdoor swimming, nature walks, and arts and crafts Job Fit Program helps adults and/or youth with LD's and/or ADHD explore interests, strengths, and areas of need in order to pursue employment covers how to ask for accommodations in the workplace ensures clients have a full understanding of their LD's and develops client's confidence to join the workforce Parent Support Group virtual support group to help guide caregivers through different stages of accepting and managing their child's Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD monthly opportunity to meet and share common experiences, handle emotions, strategize for strengths and challenges in a positive way Adult Support Group opportunity to network with individuals with LD's and/or ADHD, gain social support, problem solve and share successes. learn different strategies to deal with and manage LDs and/or ADHD.

address: 9030 Leslie St, Ste 216 Richmond Hill, ON, L4B 1G2 (12km) Clipboard
description: Programs for children, youth, and adults with learning disabilities (LDs) and/or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) including parent and adult support groups, tutoring services, advocacy and information and referral Tutoring Volunteer tutors from different diverse and educational backgrounds work one-on-one with children in grades 4-8 on: supporting students with homework help recognizing comfortable learning styles, learning strengths, and study habits which assist children in better learning assisting children with numeracy and literacy skills which need further development Homework & Beyond ​ For students who need extra support completing homework and developing social skills during the school year; f acilitators meet with students once a week in a supportive group to: assist with homework completion and understanding. build social skills, such as making friends, self-advocacy, and addressing bullying provide a positive space for learning and personal growth Social Skills 6 week program focuses on social development skills; social coaches, who are registered social service workers, engage with students on a variety of social skills; program content is based on the PEERS® curriculum for social skills training Group meets once a week for one hour and discusses topics such as: interpersonal skills. i.e. communication, listening, friendship skills coping with emotions and stress conflict resolution and problem solving self advocacy and self esteem skills SOAR Transitions Elementary for students in grades 7-8 diagnosed with LD and/or ADHD assists in developing life skills to succeed academically and vocationally SOAR Transitions Secondary -- supports students in secondary schools diagnosed with learning disability or ADHD gain practical coping and advocacy skills in one to one and group sessions Strategies for Life one-on one program that focuses on the socio-emotional effects of LDs and Attention Deficit Disorder identifies unique challenges the participant may face and assists youth in developing resiliency. Summer Camp day camp for children with a primary diagnosis of Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD safe, encouraging and positive environment for learning social skills activities include outdoor swimming, nature walks, and arts and crafts Job Fit Program helps adults and/or youth with LD's and/or ADHD explore interests, strengths, and areas of need in order to pursue employment covers how to ask for accommodations in the workplace ensures clients have a full understanding of their LD's and develops client's confidence to join the workforce Parent Support Group virtual support group to help guide caregivers through different stages of accepting and managing their child's Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD monthly opportunity to meet and share common experiences, handle emotions, strategize for strengths and challenges in a positive way Adult Support Group opportunity to network with individuals with LD's and/or ADHD, gain social support, problem solve and share successes. learn different strategies to deal with and manage LDs and/or ADHD.

description: Centre for Equitable Library Access (CELA) Resources through a partnership with the CNIB/CELA library, access the collections and services of the CNIB/CELA library collections include Braille, Talking Books and magazines, electronic text and descriptive videos available at Woodside Branch Connection Kits offering two types of connection kits, a Tablet + Wi-Fi Kit or an Accessible Reading Tablet these kits are to help Oakville residents who are 55+ and do not have access to the internet or technology, stay connected with friends and family and to access alternate formats of reading material for those with print disabilities to learn more, please call your local branch for more information DAISY Disc an audio book that uses the international DAISY (Digital Accessible Information System) Standard available at Woodside Branch Delivery by Mail if you are unable to come into the library in person, you may be eligible for OPL's Delivery Service option please email oplbymail@oakville.ca or contact your local branch to find out if you qualify restricted to a maximum of two (2) items per package, 2 packages per month per customer Large Print Books popular fiction and non-fiction books in large print format search catalogue for current large print items available at all branches MagnusCards the MagnusCards app is designed to help individuals with autism or other special needs interact independently and with confidence in the Oakville community users can follow guides to library services by downloading the app for free through the App Store or on Google Play ReachDeck Toolbar makes the OPL website more accessible with powerful features READING AIDS Kurzweil Reading Machines computers equipped with scanners and specialized software. They allow you to scan printed material, magnify it on the screen, and then have the text read aloud to you, available at Woodside and Central Branch ZoomText a screen magnification software that allows customers to view the library catalogue and the internet in large print, available at Woodside and Central Branch SmartView a magnifier that enhances all reading materials (magazines, newspapers, photographs, bills), available at Woodside Branch Victor Readers / DAISY Player also known as DAISY players, are used for listening to digital talking books, available at Woodside Branch

name: Chisholm Academy
address: 1484 Cornwall Rd Oakville, ON, L6J 7W5 (3km) Clipboard
description: A special education school that caters to students with learning disabilities such as dyslexia, ADHD and executive functioning challenges Academic programs and services include: private senior elementary school private high school individualized educational programs (IEP) academic upgrading for students and adults with learning disabilities Affiliated with the Chisholm Assessment Centre providing educational assessments and counselling services for elementary, secondary and post-secondary students

name: Oakville Public Library
address: 1274 Rebecca St Oakville, ON, L6L 1Z2 (4km) Clipboard
description: Centre for Equitable Library Access (CELA) Resources through a partnership with the CNIB/CELA library, access the collections and services of the CNIB/CELA library collections include Braille, Talking Books and magazines, electronic text and descriptive videos available at Woodside Branch Connection Kits offering two types of connection kits, a Tablet + Wi-Fi Kit or an Accessible Reading Tablet these kits are to help Oakville residents who are 55+ and do not have access to the internet or technology, stay connected with friends and family and to access alternate formats of reading material for those with print disabilities to learn more, please call your local branch for more information DAISY Disc an audio book that uses the international DAISY (Digital Accessible Information System) Standard available at Woodside Branch Delivery by Mail if you are unable to come into the library in person, you may be eligible for OPL's Delivery Service option please email oplbymail@oakville.ca or contact your local branch to find out if you qualify restricted to a maximum of two (2) items per package, 2 packages per month per customer Large Print Books popular fiction and non-fiction books in large print format search catalogue for current large print items available at all branches MagnusCards the MagnusCards app is designed to help individuals with autism or other special needs interact independently and with confidence in the Oakville community users can follow guides to library services by downloading the app for free through the App Store or on Google Play ReachDeck Toolbar makes the OPL website more accessible with powerful features READING AIDS Kurzweil Reading Machines computers equipped with scanners and specialized software. They allow you to scan printed material, magnify it on the screen, and then have the text read aloud to you, available at Woodside and Central Branch ZoomText a screen magnification software that allows customers to view the library catalogue and the internet in large print, available at Woodside and Central Branch SmartView a magnifier that enhances all reading materials (magazines, newspapers, photographs, bills), available at Woodside Branch Victor Readers / DAISY Player also known as DAISY players, are used for listening to digital talking books, available at Woodside Branch

name: Centre for Diverse Learners
address: Rotary Youth Centre; 560 Guelph Line, Top Fl Burlington, ON, L7R 3M4 (15km) Clipboard
description: Major services: operates a resource centre in Burlington offers programs to support the specific needs of children and youth with learning disabilities hosts lectures and workshops related to Learning Disabilities, ADHD, and other relevant topics holds regular parent advocacy sessions to empower parents to advocate for their children provides information and resources related to Learning Disabilities Also hosts an annual conference for educators, professionals, and parents combines peer-reviewed research with hands-on strategies for understanding and working with students with Learning Disabilities and ADHD

name: Learning Disabilities Association of Peel Region
address: 5070 Fairwind Dr, Rms 205 and 206 Mississauga, ON, L5R 2N4 (17km) Clipboard
description: Support and guidance, resource information, workshops, support groups, public awareness for children and youth with learning differences and their families Academic Support and Tutoring for Elementary and Secondary School Students Supportive Literacy Program Supportive Math Program in-person and virtual options Social Skills Promotes self-awareness and healthy behaviors through workshops on conflict resolution, healthy relationships, peer pressure, online safety, and self-esteem held in-person FRIENDS Summer Camp summer activities for neurodiverse children including outdoor activities, indoor events such as animal connections through Sunshine Therapy Dogs summer activities for children 6-12 years with learning differences who may not fit into conventional summer camps Mississauga and Brampton locations as of summer 2024, contact for information Dyslexia Support Individualized multi-sensory intervention for individuals with language-based difficulties/dyslexia Programming also offered at a second location in Brampton at St Jean-Marie Vianney Catholic Elementary School, 75 Jordensen Dr

name: Ontario. Ministry of Education
address: Trillium Demonstration School, Building H; 347 Ontario St S Milton, ON, L9T 3X9 (18km) Clipboard
description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

name: Camp Kennebec
address: 1422 Cox Rd Central Frontenac, ON, K0H 1B0 (258km) Clipboard
description: Residential camp for children and adults with special needs group rentals during off season camp runs one to eight week programs programs are adaptable to campers if and when they need it wide range of activities available leadership training Waterfront games, sports and skills: swimming waterskiing water trampoline wakeboarding tubing canoeing kayaking paddle boards Expert instruction and skills building in land activities: horseback riding high ropes zip lining archery land sports trips and camp-outs Creative and artistic options: music wood shop arts and crafts dance drama NOTE: Open to campers from all locations.

address: 240 Centrum Blvd, Unit 105 Ottawa, ON, K1E 3J4 (400km) Clipboard
description: Support organization for parents and caregivers of children with learning disabilities represents parents and their children in various fields in education, especially early childhood education raises awareness among parents and educators about learning disorders (dyslexia, dysorthography, dyscalculia, dyspraxia, dysphasia, attention deficit disorder with or without hyperactivity (ADD/ADHD) by providing information about: the symptoms linked to the different forms and levels of dyslexia and attention deficit with or without hyperactivity different teaching strategies and adaptive methods community resources encourages early detection of learning disabilities support to parents by phone, by email, and through virtual meetings, such as conferences (virtually by ZOOM) information and sharing sessions (virtually by ZOOM) training sessions for parents

program: Dyslexia Resource Centre Multi-Sensory Reading Remedy Program
address: 1175 Wilson St E, Unit B Ancaster, ON, L8S 4K6 (24km) Clipboard
description: Offers a structured language program for teachers, tutors, parents, and home schoolers, to help promote literacy and learning.  This method of instruction combines techniques with teaching the structures of written English, sounds (phonemes), meaning units (prefixes, suffixes, roots), and common spelling rules. name: Learning Disabilities Association of York-Simcoe
address: 9030 Leslie St, Ste 216 Richmond Hill, ON, L4B 1G2 (24km) Clipboard
description: Programs for children, youth, and adults with learning disabilities (LDs) and/or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) including parent and adult support groups, tutoring services, advocacy and information and referral Tutoring Volunteer tutors from different diverse and educational backgrounds work one-on-one with children in grades 4-8 on: supporting students with homework help recognizing comfortable learning styles, learning strengths, and study habits which assist children in better learning assisting children with numeracy and literacy skills which need further development Homework & Beyond ​ For students who need extra support completing homework and developing social skills during the school year; f acilitators meet with students once a week in a supportive group to: assist with homework completion and understanding. build social skills, such as making friends, self-advocacy, and addressing bullying provide a positive space for learning and personal growth Social Skills 6 week program focuses on social development skills; social coaches, who are registered social service workers, engage with students on a variety of social skills; program content is based on the PEERS® curriculum for social skills training Group meets once a week for one hour and discusses topics such as: interpersonal skills. i.e. communication, listening, friendship skills coping with emotions and stress conflict resolution and problem solving self advocacy and self esteem skills SOAR Transitions Elementary for students in grades 7-8 diagnosed with LD and/or ADHD assists in developing life skills to succeed academically and vocationally SOAR Transitions Secondary -- supports students in secondary schools diagnosed with learning disability or ADHD gain practical coping and advocacy skills in one to one and group sessions Strategies for Life one-on one program that focuses on the socio-emotional effects of LDs and Attention Deficit Disorder identifies unique challenges the participant may face and assists youth in developing resiliency. Summer Camp day camp for children with a primary diagnosis of Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD safe, encouraging and positive environment for learning social skills activities include outdoor swimming, nature walks, and arts and crafts Job Fit Program helps adults and/or youth with LD's and/or ADHD explore interests, strengths, and areas of need in order to pursue employment covers how to ask for accommodations in the workplace ensures clients have a full understanding of their LD's and develops client's confidence to join the workforce Parent Support Group virtual support group to help guide caregivers through different stages of accepting and managing their child's Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD monthly opportunity to meet and share common experiences, handle emotions, strategize for strengths and challenges in a positive way Adult Support Group opportunity to network with individuals with LD's and/or ADHD, gain social support, problem solve and share successes. learn different strategies to deal with and manage LDs and/or ADHD.

name: Northumberland Child Development Centre
address: 205 Peter St Port Hope, ON, L1A 3V6 (2km) Clipboard
description: Mosaic Minds provides integrative, therapeutic services for neurodiverse children and youth with a focus on supporting sensory, emotional, social and developmental growth. Therapy offers a supportive environment where a child experiencing challenges with sensory processing, social communication, emotional regulation or developmental transitions can grow and thrive. Program staff are certified AutPlay Practitioners and certified Play Therapists. Each child's path begins with a Sensory Processing Assessment to identify individual sensory needs and areas of strength. Additional assessment builds a tailored therapy plan incorporating sensory integration techniques, emotional regulation strategies, AutPlay therapy and other developmentally appropriate modalities. The approach, grounded in current neurodevelopmental research, blends clinical expertise with child-centered care, creating a supportive and engaging therapeutic environment. The aim is to improve functional skills, increase self regulation, foster connection and help every child succeed.

description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

name: Learning Disabilities Association of York-Simcoe
address: 9030 Leslie St, Ste 216 Richmond Hill, ON, L4B 1G2 (13km) Clipboard
description: Programs for children, youth, and adults with learning disabilities (LDs) and/or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) including parent and adult support groups, tutoring services, advocacy and information and referral Tutoring Volunteer tutors from different diverse and educational backgrounds work one-on-one with children in grades 4-8 on: supporting students with homework help recognizing comfortable learning styles, learning strengths, and study habits which assist children in better learning assisting children with numeracy and literacy skills which need further development Homework & Beyond ​ For students who need extra support completing homework and developing social skills during the school year; f acilitators meet with students once a week in a supportive group to: assist with homework completion and understanding. build social skills, such as making friends, self-advocacy, and addressing bullying provide a positive space for learning and personal growth Social Skills 6 week program focuses on social development skills; social coaches, who are registered social service workers, engage with students on a variety of social skills; program content is based on the PEERS® curriculum for social skills training Group meets once a week for one hour and discusses topics such as: interpersonal skills. i.e. communication, listening, friendship skills coping with emotions and stress conflict resolution and problem solving self advocacy and self esteem skills SOAR Transitions Elementary for students in grades 7-8 diagnosed with LD and/or ADHD assists in developing life skills to succeed academically and vocationally SOAR Transitions Secondary -- supports students in secondary schools diagnosed with learning disability or ADHD gain practical coping and advocacy skills in one to one and group sessions Strategies for Life one-on one program that focuses on the socio-emotional effects of LDs and Attention Deficit Disorder identifies unique challenges the participant may face and assists youth in developing resiliency. Summer Camp day camp for children with a primary diagnosis of Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD safe, encouraging and positive environment for learning social skills activities include outdoor swimming, nature walks, and arts and crafts Job Fit Program helps adults and/or youth with LD's and/or ADHD explore interests, strengths, and areas of need in order to pursue employment covers how to ask for accommodations in the workplace ensures clients have a full understanding of their LD's and develops client's confidence to join the workforce Parent Support Group virtual support group to help guide caregivers through different stages of accepting and managing their child's Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD monthly opportunity to meet and share common experiences, handle emotions, strategize for strengths and challenges in a positive way Adult Support Group opportunity to network with individuals with LD's and/or ADHD, gain social support, problem solve and share successes. learn different strategies to deal with and manage LDs and/or ADHD.

address: 9030 Leslie St, Ste 216 Richmond Hill, ON, L4B 1G2 (17km) Clipboard
description: Programs for children, youth, and adults with learning disabilities (LDs) and/or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) including parent and adult support groups, tutoring services, advocacy and information and referral Tutoring Volunteer tutors from different diverse and educational backgrounds work one-on-one with children in grades 4-8 on: supporting students with homework help recognizing comfortable learning styles, learning strengths, and study habits which assist children in better learning assisting children with numeracy and literacy skills which need further development Homework & Beyond ​ For students who need extra support completing homework and developing social skills during the school year; f acilitators meet with students once a week in a supportive group to: assist with homework completion and understanding. build social skills, such as making friends, self-advocacy, and addressing bullying provide a positive space for learning and personal growth Social Skills 6 week program focuses on social development skills; social coaches, who are registered social service workers, engage with students on a variety of social skills; program content is based on the PEERS® curriculum for social skills training Group meets once a week for one hour and discusses topics such as: interpersonal skills. i.e. communication, listening, friendship skills coping with emotions and stress conflict resolution and problem solving self advocacy and self esteem skills SOAR Transitions Elementary for students in grades 7-8 diagnosed with LD and/or ADHD assists in developing life skills to succeed academically and vocationally SOAR Transitions Secondary -- supports students in secondary schools diagnosed with learning disability or ADHD gain practical coping and advocacy skills in one to one and group sessions Strategies for Life one-on one program that focuses on the socio-emotional effects of LDs and Attention Deficit Disorder identifies unique challenges the participant may face and assists youth in developing resiliency. Summer Camp day camp for children with a primary diagnosis of Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD safe, encouraging and positive environment for learning social skills activities include outdoor swimming, nature walks, and arts and crafts Job Fit Program helps adults and/or youth with LD's and/or ADHD explore interests, strengths, and areas of need in order to pursue employment covers how to ask for accommodations in the workplace ensures clients have a full understanding of their LD's and develops client's confidence to join the workforce Parent Support Group virtual support group to help guide caregivers through different stages of accepting and managing their child's Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD monthly opportunity to meet and share common experiences, handle emotions, strategize for strengths and challenges in a positive way Adult Support Group opportunity to network with individuals with LD's and/or ADHD, gain social support, problem solve and share successes. learn different strategies to deal with and manage LDs and/or ADHD.

description: A residential demonstration school for students with severe learning disabilities that also provides in-service teacher education and consultative services to school boards Main Services: School program an intensive and specialized program focusing on cognitive/language development for students with severe learning disabilities at elementary and secondary levels goals and objectives are set in all courses and there is a variety of credit courses offered in the secondary program assistive technology strategies are emphasized initial consultations initiated by student's home school Residence program (Mon-Fri) a structured program including individual and group counselling, instruction in social skills and recreational activities for students with severe learning disabilities In-service program designed for Ontario educators only one day workshops offered on learning disabilities and related topics that vary annually (see website in September for new list) one day visiting teacher programs allowing teachers to tour school and classes Resources Services program designed for Ontario school boards to assist them in developing programs for students with learning disabilities educational assessments for students who are potential candidates for Trillium program The Trillium Demonstration School serves as a catchment area inclusive of southern, central and mid-northern Ontario

name: International Dyslexia Association - Ontario Branch
address: Clipboard
description: IDA Ontario is primarily volunteer-operated and provides a wide range of programs including: The Literacy and Learning Conference Workshops, webinars and teacher training courses Information & support for parents navigating the school system Website with bilingual resources for libraries, teachers, parents & students Resources and support for public libraries Reading Road Trip Podcast News & updates on literacy in Ontario

address: 1422 Cox Rd Central Frontenac, ON, K0H 1B0 (200km) Clipboard

description: One-to-one tutoring for elementary aged children using individualized programs. name: Learning Disabilities Association of York-Simcoe
address: 9030 Leslie St, Ste 216 Richmond Hill, ON, L4B 1G2 (22km) Clipboard
description: Programs for children, youth, and adults with learning disabilities (LDs) and/or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) including parent and adult support groups, tutoring services, advocacy and information and referral Tutoring Volunteer tutors from different diverse and educational backgrounds work one-on-one with children in grades 4-8 on: supporting students with homework help recognizing comfortable learning styles, learning strengths, and study habits which assist children in better learning assisting children with numeracy and literacy skills which need further development Homework & Beyond ​ For students who need extra support completing homework and developing social skills during the school year; f acilitators meet with students once a week in a supportive group to: assist with homework completion and understanding. build social skills, such as making friends, self-advocacy, and addressing bullying provide a positive space for learning and personal growth Social Skills 6 week program focuses on social development skills; social coaches, who are registered social service workers, engage with students on a variety of social skills; program content is based on the PEERS® curriculum for social skills training Group meets once a week for one hour and discusses topics such as: interpersonal skills. i.e. communication, listening, friendship skills coping with emotions and stress conflict resolution and problem solving self advocacy and self esteem skills SOAR Transitions Elementary for students in grades 7-8 diagnosed with LD and/or ADHD assists in developing life skills to succeed academically and vocationally SOAR Transitions Secondary -- supports students in secondary schools diagnosed with learning disability or ADHD gain practical coping and advocacy skills in one to one and group sessions Strategies for Life one-on one program that focuses on the socio-emotional effects of LDs and Attention Deficit Disorder identifies unique challenges the participant may face and assists youth in developing resiliency. Summer Camp day camp for children with a primary diagnosis of Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD safe, encouraging and positive environment for learning social skills activities include outdoor swimming, nature walks, and arts and crafts Job Fit Program helps adults and/or youth with LD's and/or ADHD explore interests, strengths, and areas of need in order to pursue employment covers how to ask for accommodations in the workplace ensures clients have a full understanding of their LD's and develops client's confidence to join the workforce Parent Support Group virtual support group to help guide caregivers through different stages of accepting and managing their child's Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD monthly opportunity to meet and share common experiences, handle emotions, strategize for strengths and challenges in a positive way Adult Support Group opportunity to network with individuals with LD's and/or ADHD, gain social support, problem solve and share successes. learn different strategies to deal with and manage LDs and/or ADHD.

description: LDATD offers a wide variety of programs for children (ages 5-13) inculding: social skills development academic support navigating assistive technologies typing classes LDATD offers a wide variety of programs for youth (ages 15-30) including: academic support after-school programming civic engagement opportunities employment programming/support

name: Learning Disabilities Association of Toronto District
address: 121 Willowdale Ave, Suite 103 Toronto, ON, M2N 6A3 (11km) Clipboard
description: Committed to creating a society in which people with Learning Disabilities (LDs) are able to achieve the full expression of their intellect and abilities and enabled to contribute in the workplace to their fullest Providing: Information about learning disabilities and Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) Support and guidance to parents and guardians concerning their children's rights in the school system Advocacy for both children and adults Information to community groups, schools, professional development days, conferences and seminars. Referral services Direct programming for children, youth and adults Services and Programs: Adult Programs - Support for adults with Learning Disabilities / AD(H)D Children and Youth Programs Resource, Information and Advocacy Y.I.E.L.D Program Youth H.I.R.E (Help in Reaching Employment) Program

name: Learning Disabilities Association of York-Simcoe
address: 9030 Leslie St, Ste 216 Richmond Hill, ON, L4B 1G2 (13km) Clipboard
description: Programs for children, youth, and adults with learning disabilities (LDs) and/or Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) including parent and adult support groups, tutoring services, advocacy and information and referral Tutoring Volunteer tutors from different diverse and educational backgrounds work one-on-one with children in grades 4-8 on: supporting students with homework help recognizing comfortable learning styles, learning strengths, and study habits which assist children in better learning assisting children with numeracy and literacy skills which need further development Homework & Beyond ​ For students who need extra support completing homework and developing social skills during the school year; f acilitators meet with students once a week in a supportive group to: assist with homework completion and understanding. build social skills, such as making friends, self-advocacy, and addressing bullying provide a positive space for learning and personal growth Social Skills 6 week program focuses on social development skills; social coaches, who are registered social service workers, engage with students on a variety of social skills; program content is based on the PEERS® curriculum for social skills training Group meets once a week for one hour and discusses topics such as: interpersonal skills. i.e. communication, listening, friendship skills coping with emotions and stress conflict resolution and problem solving self advocacy and self esteem skills SOAR Transitions Elementary for students in grades 7-8 diagnosed with LD and/or ADHD assists in developing life skills to succeed academically and vocationally SOAR Transitions Secondary -- supports students in secondary schools diagnosed with learning disability or ADHD gain practical coping and advocacy skills in one to one and group sessions Strategies for Life one-on one program that focuses on the socio-emotional effects of LDs and Attention Deficit Disorder identifies unique challenges the participant may face and assists youth in developing resiliency. Summer Camp day camp for children with a primary diagnosis of Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD safe, encouraging and positive environment for learning social skills activities include outdoor swimming, nature walks, and arts and crafts Job Fit Program helps adults and/or youth with LD's and/or ADHD explore interests, strengths, and areas of need in order to pursue employment covers how to ask for accommodations in the workplace ensures clients have a full understanding of their LD's and develops client's confidence to join the workforce Parent Support Group virtual support group to help guide caregivers through different stages of accepting and managing their child's Learning Disabilities and/or ADHD monthly opportunity to meet and share common experiences, handle emotions, strategize for strengths and challenges in a positive way Adult Support Group opportunity to network with individuals with LD's and/or ADHD, gain social support, problem solve and share successes. learn different strategies to deal with and manage LDs and/or ADHD.

name: Child Development Institute
program: Learning Disabilities and Mental Health Services
address: 46 St Clair Gardens Toronto, ON, M6E 3V4 (14km) Clipboard
description: Children's mental health services, including assessment, planning and case coordination, clinical services and treatment groups for children and youth, individual counselling, parenting and family counselling, and parent support groups workshops, community education and engagement, and consultation to schools, social agencies and community groups Towhee Summer Residential Program: residential therapeutic summer camp for children and youth 10-18 years

name: Ontario Institute for Studies in Education of the University of Toronto
address: 252 Bloor St W, Suite 7-296 Toronto, ON, M5S 1V6 (17km) Clipboard
description: Psychological services: assessment and psychotherapeutic services services are partly provided by supervised graduate students topics addressed include emotional, social, interpersonal, and academic issues Children and adolescent services also encompass academic interventions and counselling and guidance for parents
